'''Ank Ki'Shor''' was a [[Factory world/Legends|manufacturing world]]<ref name="Xim Week" /> located in the [[Ank Ki'Shor system/Legends|Ank Ki'Shor system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']]<ref name="EAOC" /> [[Indrexu sector/Legends|Indrexu sector]]. Situated within the historical [[Greater Tion]] region, it lay on the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]]<ref name="Xim Week" /> [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> which connected it to the [[Estaria system/Legends|Estaria]] and [[Janodral Mizar system/Legends|Janodral Mizar systems]]<ref name="Xim Week" /> and served as the boundary between the regions known as the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] and [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]].<ref name="Regions" /> The inhabitants of Ank Ki'Shor were not particular as to who they did business with, and during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] existence the [[Planet/Legends|world]] became a key supplier of that organization.<ref name="Xim Week" />

In the early phase of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="NEGVV">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref> the [[Siege of Ank Ki'Shor]] took place at the world.<ref name="Databank">{{DB|starship|ywingstarfighter|Y-wing starfighter}}</ref> Although the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] ultimately emerged victorious in that engagement,<ref name="TEA" /> Rebel [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighters]] nevertheless defended Ank Ki'Shor's population against Imperial bombardment,<ref name="Xim Week" /> saving thousands of lives by holding off the Empire's attacking [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bomber]] [[Bomber/Legends|craft]].<ref name="Databank" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ank Ki'Shor was introduced via a mention of the Siege of Ank Ki'Shor in the [[1996]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' by [[Bill Smith]].<ref name="EGTVV">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref> The [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry for the Y-wing [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] subsequently misspelled the name as the Siege of Ank Ki'Shar.<ref name="Databank" /> The [[December 7]], [[2009]] StarWars.com [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]<ref name="XimOriginal">{{SWArchive|url=fans/hyperspace/source/ximweek01/index.html|text=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}}</ref> article "[[Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster]]" by [[Jason Fry]] first identified Ank Ki'Shor as a distinct world,<ref name="Xim Week" /> and the [[August 18]], 2009<ref name="Penguin">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/185066/the-essential-atlas-star-wars-by-daniel-wallace-and-jason-fry|text=The Essential Atlas: Star Wars}}</ref> reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Ank Ki'Shor [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Ank Ki'Shor itself, in grid square S-5.<ref name="TEA AnkKiShor" />
